common.privacyNotice: Your API key and data are stored locally in your browser to ensure privacy. If you have any questions or suggestions, please contact us via the community or feedback page.

common.useCacheTooltip: Reuse previous translation cache to speed up processing

common.multiLanguageModeTooltip: Translate to multiple languages simultaneously, saved as separate files
common.resetUploadTooltip: Clear current input and uploaded files
common.sameLanguageTarget: Source and target languages are the same; automatically adjusted target to

common.llmUnavailable: LLM node unavailable, please check configuration in 'API Settings'
common.noFileUploaded: Please upload a file to process first

common.noSourceText: Please enter or paste text content
common.formatTooltip: Format: remove blank lines and trim leading/trailing spaces
common.resultToSourceTooltip: Overwrite input area with result text
common.readOnlyModeTooltip: Text is too long; automatically switched to read-only mode to maintain page performance
common.clearInputTooltip: Clear input content and uploaded files
common.removeCharsAfterTranslationTooltip: Automatically remove specified characters after translation (separate multiple with spaces)
common.multiLangSettingsHint: Enter language codes, separated by comma or space.
common.retryCountTooltip: Number of retry attempts when translation fails
common.requestTimeoutSecTooltip: Maximum wait per request (seconds). LLM thinking takes longer; 180s or more recommended.
common.customExportFilenameTooltip: '{'name'}' = original filename, '{'lang'}' = language, '{'ext'}' = extension, '{'date'}' = date, '{'time'}' = time
common.partialFailureDesc: Auto-retry couldn't recover them. Failed lines kept the original text. Click Retry (successful lines are served from cache) or view the failed lines to handle them manually.
common.gtxFreeAPIUnavailable: The free Google Translate API (GTX) is currently unavailable. Please check your network connection.
common.translategemmaUnavailable: TranslateGemma node unavailable. Please verify the local server (LM Studio / llama.cpp / Ollama) is running and the URL is correct.

TextSplitter.clientDescription: Smartly split long text by character count or sentence. Perfect for AI input, social media, and editing. For non-UTF-8 text (e.g., some French files), upload file directly for auto-encoding detection.
TextSplitter.noExportableText: No exportable content detected, please check input
TextSplitter.emptyForSymbolOnly: Set to 0 or leave empty: ignore length and split by the delimiter below
TextSplitter.splitByLengthHint: Chunk text into blocks of a character count. If you fill delimiters below, boundaries snap to the nearest one so sentences aren't cut mid-way.
TextSplitter.splitByChineseParagraphTooltip: Smart split based on Chinese paragraph rules (newlines)
TextSplitter.splitByEnglishParagraphTooltip: Smart split based on English sentence algorithms
TextSplitter.checkDownloadDirectory: All split files exported, check download directory

textSplitter.title: Text Splitter
textSplitter.description: Smart long text splitter for AI and social media
textSplitter.metaTitle: Text Splitter | Smart Chunker for ChatGPT, Claude & Twitter
textSplitter.metaDescription: Text too long for AI? Smartly split content by chars or sentences for ChatGPT & social media. Auto-detect paragraphs, merge results & export. Fix limits now!

faq.q: What are the character limits when splitting text for ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and translation tools?
faq.a: AI model context limits (tokens): ChatGPT GPT-4o ~128K, Claude 3.5/3.7 Sonnet 200K, Gemini 1.5/2.0 Pro 1M, DeepSeek V3 128K. Chinese is ~1-2 chars/token (Claude more generous), English ~4 chars/token. Translation limits: Google Translate and DeepL free both 5000 chars/request. Set 'Split Character Count' with buffer below the target AI's token capacity.

faq.q: How do I split long posts for Twitter/X, Threads, Mastodon, or Bluesky?
faq.a: Platform limits: Twitter/X 280 chars, Threads 500, Mastodon 500, Bluesky 300, LinkedIn 3000, Tumblr 4096, Reddit posts 40000, Substack notes 500. Use 'English paragraph' mode for Latin scripts — preserves sentence integrity at word boundaries. Batch-export as separate files for sequential posting (1/N, 2/N numbering).

faq.q: How do I handle mixed Chinese/English text or non-UTF-8 files (French GBK, Japanese Shift-JIS)?
faq.a: For mixed CJK/English, use Chinese paragraph mode — it recognizes both Chinese (。！？) and English (. ! ?) sentence endings without splitting mid-word. Custom delimiter supports escape chars \\n\\n and \\t. For non-UTF-8 files (French GBK, Japanese Shift-JIS, Korean EUC-KR), **upload the file directly** rather than pasting — encoding is auto-detected to prevent garbled output.

howto.name: Input long text into the tool
howto.text: Paste very long text (novel chapters, speeches, research papers) or upload TXT file. For non-UTF-8 files (French GBK, Japanese Shift-JIS, Korean EUC-KR), upload the file directly for auto encoding detection.

howto.name: Set character count by AI model or platform
howto.text: ChatGPT free 4000, Plus 32K, Claude 200K, Gemini 1M; Twitter/X 280, Threads 500, Mastodon 500, Bluesky 300, LinkedIn 3000. Configure character count with buffer below the target limit.

howto.name: Export for AI input or social posting
howto.text: Copy single segments directly into ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini. Batch-export as separate files numbered 1/N, 2/N for sequential posting on Twitter/Threads/Mastodon/LinkedIn. Merged export for saving organized long-form text.

features: AI model context limits: ChatGPT GPT-4o 128K tokens, Claude 3.5/3.7 Sonnet 200K, Gemini 1.5/2.0 Pro 1M, DeepSeek V3 128K
Social platform templates: Twitter 280, Threads 500, Mastodon 500, Bluesky 300, LinkedIn 3000
Smart punctuation detection for mixed CJK (。！？) and Latin (. ! ?) text
Non-UTF-8 auto-detection (GBK, Shift-JIS, EUC-KR) prevents garbled output
Custom delimiter with \\n\\n, \\t escape character support
Batch export numbered 1/N, 2/N for sequential posting
